title: rubus thyrsoideus subsp. armoricus
intro: Rubus thyrsoideus subsp. armoricus, commonly known as Armorican bramble, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family, Rubusaceae. It is native to Europe and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues and inflammation. The plant contains anthraquinones and other bioactive compounds that exhibit antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, providing health benefits such as relieving digestive discomfort, reducing inflammation, and supporting cardiovascular health. Rubus thyrsoideus subsp. armoricus can be prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also used in herbal teas and infusions to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
